## 3. Major Financial AI Company News
- 2026-03-27 | [Orbital Data-Center Startup Aetherflux Raising New Financing at $2 Billion Valuation](https://www.wsj.com/articles/orbital-data-center-startup-aetherflux-raising-new-financing-at-2-billion-valuation-140a4073) | Aetherflux is reportedly raising roughly $250 million to $300 million at a $2 billion valuation for solar-powered orbital data centers aimed at AI workloads. Why it matters: capital continues to move upstream into power-and-compute infrastructure, not just models and applications.

## 4. Major AI Data Center News
- 2026-03-27 | [Microsoft takes over a Texas AI data center expansion after OpenAI backs away](https://apnews.com/article/f4f74c3a4617d8cfab5b933fc31ccc6e) | Microsoft is taking over two new Crusoe-built AI data center buildings and an on-site 900MW power plant in Abilene, Texas, next to the OpenAI/Oracle Stargate campus. Why it matters: control of a major new AI compute site is shifting, with immediate implications for frontier-model training capacity and infrastructure alignment.

## 9. Podcast Highlights
- 2026-03-27 | [Why AI Needs Better Benchmarks](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kLZeFWZewH0) | Topic: ARC AGI 3 and interactive reasoning benchmarks. Available now: the episode describes ARC AGI 3 as newly launched. How to use it: evaluate agent-style systems on interactive, multi-step tasks rather than relying on single-shot knowledge scores alone. Why it matters: static benchmark gains are becoming less useful for discriminating among frontier models.
- 2026-03-27 | [Why AI Needs Better Benchmarks](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kLZeFWZewH0) | Topic: benchmark saturation across MMLU, GPQA, SWE-bench, Humanity's Last Exam, and TerminalBench. Available now: these benchmark families are in active use across model announcements. How to use it: treat vendor benchmark claims as directional; validate models with task-specific evals, tool-use tests, and live workflows before deployment. Why it matters: shrinking score gaps can mask real-world differences in reliability and agent performance.
- 2026-03-27 | [Why AI Needs Better Benchmarks](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kLZeFWZewH0) | Topic: benchmark maxing. Available now: the issue is current wherever labs can train toward known or semi-public tests. How to use it: prefer evaluation sets that are private, refreshed, or operationally realistic, and monitor for divergence between leaderboard scores and field results. Why it matters: benchmark-optimized models can overstate real capability, especially in coding and agentic use cases.
